steps.title: Spot the opening em dash and the parenthetical phrase
steps.content: The sentence contains "Interpol—the International Criminal Police ______" — an em dash opens a parenthetical phrase that defines what Interpol is. A parenthetical phrase opened by an em dash must be closed by a matching em dash.

steps.title: Apply the matching em-dash rule
steps.content:
Rule: em dash in, em dash out. Test the reconstructed sentence with each choice:

A) Organization, → "Interpol—the International Criminal Police Organization, in 1971" — the em dash is never closed; a comma cannot close an em-dash parenthetical. Fails.

B) Organization— → "Interpol—the International Criminal Police Organization—in 1971" — the em dash is properly closed, neatly bracketing the definition. Correct.

C) Organization → "Interpol—the International Criminal Police Organization in 1971" — the em dash is never closed; the parenthetical bleeds into the rest of the sentence. Fails.

D) Organization: → "Interpol—the International Criminal Police Organization: in 1971" — a colon cannot close an em-dash parenthetical, and "in 1971" would incorrectly appear to follow the colon as a list. Fails.
